attachment.content:
Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# WP-011 — KRN-V3-03: Multi-roof Composition + Fascia/Gutter + Soffit
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof`
4	**Effort:** XL — 4 weeks (1.5 w G11 + 1 w G12 + 0.5 w G13 + 1 w shared CSG / preview / mitering)
5	**State:** `now`
6	
7	## Required reading
8	
9	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T1 KRN-V3-03 entry (around line 573)
10	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
11	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — `RoofElem` (line 576), `SweepElem` (line 751), `DormerElem` (line 777), `ElementKind` union (line 1492)
12	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `CreateRoofCmd` (line 479), `AttachWallTopToRoofCmd` (line 503), `CreateRoofOpeningCmd` (line 574), `CreateDormerCmd` (line 1294)
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — roof dispatch block: `case CreateRoofCmd` (line 1888), `case AttachWallTopToRoofCmd` (line 1943), `case CreateRoofOpeningCmd` (line 1999), `case CreateDormerCmd` (line 2202); `apply_inplace` (line 886); `try_commit_bundle` (line 4765)
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py` — all helpers: `RoofGeometryMode`, `gable_ridge_segment_plan_mm`, `gable_rectangle_fascia_edge_plan_token_v0`, convexity + L-shape predicates
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `roof` TS type (line 603), `sweep` TS type (line 1128)
16	
17	## WP-011 verbatim from tracker (§6 T1 KRN-V3-03)
18	
19	**KRN-V3-03 — Multi-roof composition + fascia/gutter + soffit (bundled)**
20	
21	_Source: R-D §3 G11 + G12 + G13. **Bundled triple — they ship together as the "looks finished" gate.** Without all three, presentation drawings of pitched-roof houses look unfinished._
22	
23	**Status.** `now`.
24	**Scope.** Three coupled WPs that ship as one bundle because together they are the "B1 looks-finished" gate. **(a)** Multi-roof composition via `RoofJoin` operator: pick two roofs → resolve intersection into a watertight composite (CSG-stitched). Most ≥3-bedroom houses are gable+hip+shed combinations. **(b)** Fascia / gutter / downpipe: roof-attached profile runs along eave / rake edges. Generalises into an `EdgeProfileRun` pattern that also serves G2 wall sweeps (KRN-V3-08). **(c)** Soffit: horizontal under-side of a roof eave, sketched 2D boundary + thickness, lives between roof and wall top.
25	
26	**Data model.**
27	```ts
28	// (a) RoofJoin
29	{
30	  kind: 'roof_join';
31	  primaryRoofId: string;
32	  secondaryRoofId: string;
33	  seamMode: 'clip_secondary_into_primary' | 'merge_at_ridge';
34	}
35	
36	// (b) EdgeProfileRun
37	{
38	  kind: 'edge_profile_run';
39	  hostElementId: string;            // roof | wall | floor
40	  hostEdge: 'eave' | 'rake' | 'ridge' | 'top' | 'bottom' | { startMm; endMm };
41	  profileFamilyId: string;          // 2D profile (cornice, fascia, gutter, plinth, water-table)
42	  offsetMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number };
43	  miterMode: 'auto' | 'manual';
44	}
45	
46	// (c) Soffit
47	{
48	  kind: 'soffit';
49	  boundary: Array<{ xMm: number; yMm: number }>;   // 2D polygon in plan
50	  hostRoofId?: string;
51	  thicknessMm: number;
52	  zMm: number;
53	}
54	```
55	**Engine.** **(a)** `RoofJoin` apply runs Three.js / OpenCASCADE-style CSG against the two roof solids; result is a derived `roof_join` element that renders the seam. Validation: roofs must intersect; no degenerate seams. UX: select two → "Join Roofs" → show seam in `--draft-warning` orange before commit (U3 CSG-preview pattern). **(b)** `EdgeProfileRun` solves a swept solid: 2D profile × edge polyline → 3D solid. Auto-mitres at corners (≤ tolerance). Renders in plan as a thin line, in elevation / 3D as the swept solid. **(c)** Soffit is solved as a thickened 2D extrusion; snap-to-eave-edge helper places it correctly under the roof overhang.
56	
57	**UI.** New tools in the right rail: "Join Roofs" (prompts for two roofs); "Edge Profile" (prompts for host edge + profile family — fascia, gutter, downpipe, plinth, cornice, water-table all ship as profile families); "Soffit" (sketch-mode polygon + thickness). The pre-commit CSG preview is the marquee UX moment.
58	
59	**Acceptance.** Demo seed has a gable over the main mass + a hip over an L-extension. Pre-T1: the two roofs collide visibly at the join. Post-T1: select both → "Join Roofs" → preview seam in orange → commit; the join reads watertight from any 3D angle. Then: select the eave edge → "Edge Profile" → fascia → run in 60 fps. Then: sketch a soffit polygon under the eave → "Soffit" 50 mm thick → ships. Final demo angle: warm-shadow render of the full house with no naked edges anywhere.
60	
61	**Effort.** XL — 4 weeks (1.5 weeks G11 + 1 week G12 + 0.5 week G13 + 1 week shared CSG / preview / mitering plumbing).
62	
63	**Cross-theme references.** T2 (CSG-preview pattern is reusable for floor/wall joins; U3 lives here); T5 (profile families catalog: fascia, gutter, downpipe, plinth, cornice, water-table); T4 (multi-roof composition is what makes 3D presentation drawings publishable).
64	
65	## Setup
66	
67	```bash
68	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
69	git checkout -b feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof
70	```
71	
72	## Implementation tasks
73	
74	### Part A — RoofJoin (G11, 1.5 weeks)
75	
76	1. **Add `RoofJoinElem` to `elements.py`.**
77	   - `kind: Literal["roof_join"]`, fields: `primary_roof_id: str`, `secondary_roof_id: str`, `seam_mode: Literal["clip_secondary_into_primary", "merge_at_ridge"]`.
78	   - Add `"roof_join"` to the `ElementKind` literal and the `Element` discriminated union (follow the pattern of `DormerElem`).
79	   - Add `pinned: bool = False` and `phase_created / phase_demolished` stubs for IFC parity.
80	
81	2. **Add `CreateRoofJoinCmd` to `commands.py`.**
82	   - Fields: `type: Literal["createRoofJoin"]`, `id: str | None = None`, `primary_roof_id: str`, `secondary_roof_id: str`, `seam_mode`.
83	   - Import from `elements.py` as needed. Register the command in the `Command` union at the bottom of the file.
84	
85	3. **Dispatch `case CreateRoofJoinCmd()` in `engine.py`** (insert after `case CreateRoofOpeningCmd()` around line 1999).
86	   - Validate: both IDs resolve to `RoofElem`; primary ≠ secondary; footprints overlap (compute AABB intersection — non-empty is sufficient for MVP).
87	   - On degenerate / non-intersecting footprints raise `ValueError("createRoofJoin: roof footprints do not intersect")`.
88	   - Emit a `RoofJoinElem`; do NOT mutate the source `RoofElem` records — the join is a derived overlay element.
89	
90	4. **CSG preview in the renderer** (Three.js side).
91	   - When a `roof_join` element is in `PENDING` state (pre-commit dry-run), render the seam polyline in `--draft-warning` CSS token colour (do not hardcode hex).
92	   - Post-commit: render the seam as a thin ridge line using the primary roof's `materialKey`.
93	
94	5. **Add `RoofJoinElem` to the TS union in `packages/core/src/index.ts`** following the same JSDoc + field pattern as the `roof_opening` entry (line 672).
95	
96	### Part B — EdgeProfileRun (G12, 1 week)
97	
98	6. **Add `EdgeProfileRunElem` to `elements.py`.**
99	   - Fields: `kind: Literal["edge_profile_run"]`, `host_element_id: str`, `host_edge` (use a `Union[Literal["eave","rake","ridge","top","bottom"], dict]` typed as `Any` for the custom `{startMm, endMm}` variant), `profile_family_id: str`, `offset_mm: Vec2Mm`, `miter_mode: Literal["auto", "manual"]`.
100	   - Add to `ElementKind` and `Element` union.
101	
102	7. **Add `CreateEdgeProfileRunCmd` to `commands.py`.** Mirror the field layout from `EdgeProfileRunElem`. Register in the `Command` union.
103	
104	8. **Dispatch `case CreateEdgeProfileRunCmd()` in `engine.py`.**
105	   - Validate: `hostElementId` resolves; `profileFamilyId` is a non-empty string (catalog lookup deferred to T5 — accept any non-empty string for now); `miterMode` is one of the two literals.
106	   - Resolve the host edge polyline: for `RoofElem` + `hostEdge="eave"`, use `gable_rectangle_fascia_edge_plan_token_v0` from `roof_geometry.py` to derive the eave edge pairs; for `hostEdge="rake"`, use the corresponding gable rake edges.
107	   - Emit an `EdgeProfileRunElem`.
108	
109	9. **Swept-solid geometry helper in `roof_geometry.py`.**
110	   - Add `edge_profile_run_path_mm(roof: RoofElem, host_edge: str) -> list[tuple[float, float, float]]` — returns 3D waypoints for the profile path from roof geometry. Keep it pure (no element dict lookups — caller passes the resolved `RoofElem`).
111	   - Auto-mitre: at each interior corner of the path, compute the bisector angle and set the miter trim length.
112	
113	10. **TS type** for `edge_profile_run` in `packages/core/src/index.ts`.
114	
115	### Part C — Soffit (G13, 0.5 weeks)
116	
117	11. **Add `SoffitElem` to `elements.py`.**
118	    - Fields: `kind: Literal["soffit"]`, `boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm]`, `host_roof_id: str | None`, `thickness_mm: float`, `z_mm: float`.
119	    - `boundary_mm` must have ≥ 3 vertices (add `model_validator`).
120	    - Add to `ElementKind` and `Element` union.
121	
122	12. **Add `CreateSoffitCmd` to `commands.py`.**
123	
124	13. **Dispatch `case CreateSoffitCmd()` in `engine.py`.**
125	    - Validate ≥ 3 boundary vertices; `thicknessMm > 0`; if `hostRoofId` is set, it must resolve to a `RoofElem`.
126	    - Snap-to-eave helper: if `hostRoofId` is provided and `zMm` is not supplied, compute `z_mm` as the eave elevation derived from the host roof's `referenceLevelId` elevation (look up in `els`).
127	    - Emit a `SoffitElem`.
128	
129	14. **TS type** for `soffit` in `packages/core/src/index.ts`.
130	
131	### Shared / plumbing (1 week)
132	
133	15. **Write `app/tests/test_roof_join.py`** — ≥ 8 tests:
134	    - Happy path: two intersecting gable roofs → `roof_join` emitted.
135	    - Rejects identical `primaryRoofId` == `secondaryRoofId`.
136	    - Rejects non-intersecting footprints (AABB no-overlap).
137	    - `seam_mode` round-trips through `RoofJoinElem`.
138	
139	16. **Write `app/tests/test_edge_profile_run.py`** — ≥ 6 tests:
140	    - Happy path: fascia on eave edge of a gable roof.
141	    - Rejects unknown `hostElementId`.
142	    - Rejects empty `profileFamilyId`.
143	    - `auto` vs `manual` miterMode stored correctly.
144	
145	17. **Write `app/tests/test_soffit.py`** — ≥ 6 tests:
146	    - Happy path with explicit `zMm`.
147	    - Snap-to-eave: `zMm` derived from host roof when omitted.
148	    - Rejects < 3 boundary vertices.
149	    - Rejects `thicknessMm <= 0`.
150	
151	## Implementation rules
152	
153	- **API-V3-01 contract:** every new command must also register a `ToolDescriptor` in `app/bim_ai/api/` if that registry already exists; if it does not exist yet, add a `# TODO API-V3-01` comment stub next to each new `case` block in `engine.py`.
154	- **No hex literals in chrome:** the CSG-preview seam colour must use `var(--draft-warning)` CSS token — never a hardcoded `#` value. Profile run and soffit geometry colours must likewise use material tokens from T5, not inline hex.
155	- **Pure geometry helpers:** functions added to `roof_geometry.py` must remain pure (no side effects, no `els` dict access). Pass resolved elements as arguments.
156	- **ElementKind exhaustiveness:** after adding the three new `kind` literals, run `python -c "from bim_ai.elements import Element"` — any missing discriminator will raise immediately.
157	- **Do not widen `RoofElem`:** these three elements are _separate_ from `RoofElem`. Never add `roofJoinId`, `soffitIds`, etc. to `RoofElem`; use the host-reference pattern (`hostRoofId`) instead.
158	- **Seam rendering uses derived geometry only:** `RoofJoinElem` stores only references and `seamMode`; the renderer computes the seam polyline on the fly from the two `RoofElem` footprints.
159	
160	## engine.py overlap warning
161	
162	**WP-004 (dormer CSG) may have already merged into `main` before this branch was cut.** The dormer dispatch block (`case CreateDormerCmd()`, currently around line 2202) and the `_dormer_host_floor` helper (around line 555) both touch roof element lookups. Before adding the three new `case` blocks:
163	
164	1. `git rebase origin/main` and resolve any conflicts in the roof dispatch region (lines ~1888–2250).
165	2. Do not disturb the `CreateDormerCmd` case — add `CreateRoofJoinCmd`, `CreateEdgeProfileRunCmd`, `CreateSoffitCmd` immediately after `CreateRoofOpeningCmd` (line ~1999), before `CreateDormerCmd`.
166	3. After rebasing, re-run `make verify` before writing any new code to confirm the baseline is clean.
167	
168	## Verify gate
169	
170	```bash
171	# Python tests
172	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app
173	python -m pytest tests/test_roof_join.py tests/test_edge_profile_run.py tests/test_soffit.py -v
174	
175	# Full suite must stay green
176	make verify
177	
178	# TypeScript must type-check cleanly
179	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
180	pnpm tsc --noEmit
181	
182	# ElementKind smoke-check
183	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app
184	python -c "from bim_ai.elements import Element; print('ElementKind OK')"
185	```
186	
187	## Commit and push
188	
189	```bash
190	git add app/bim_ai/elements.py \
191	        app/bim_ai/commands.py \
192	        app/bim_ai/engine.py \
193	        app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py \
194	        app/tests/test_roof_join.py \
195	        app/tests/test_edge_profile_run.py \
196	        app/tests/test_soffit.py \
197	        packages/core/src/index.ts
198	
199	git commit -m "feat(krn): KRN-V3-03 — RoofJoin + EdgeProfileRun + Soffit (G11/G12/G13)"
200	git push -u origin feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof
201	```
202	
203	## Final report
204	
205	Paste this back to the orchestrator when done:
206	
207	```
208	WP: WP-011 / KRN-V3-03
209	Branch: feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof
210	Status: done
211	Tests: <N> passed  (roof_join: X, edge_profile_run: X, soffit: X)
212	Files changed: <list key files>
213	Notes: <anything unexpected — seam accuracy, miter edge cases, TS union changes>
214	```

# WP-011 — KRN-V3-03: Multi-roof Composition + Fascia/Gutter + Soffit

**Branch:** `feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof`
**Effort:** XL — 4 weeks (1.5 w G11 + 1 w G12 + 0.5 w G13 + 1 w shared CSG / preview / mitering)
**State:** `now`

## Required reading

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T1 KRN-V3-03 entry (around line 573)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — `RoofElem` (line 576), `SweepElem` (line 751), `DormerElem` (line 777), `ElementKind` union (line 1492)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `CreateRoofCmd` (line 479), `AttachWallTopToRoofCmd` (line 503), `CreateRoofOpeningCmd` (line 574), `CreateDormerCmd` (line 1294)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — roof dispatch block: `case CreateRoofCmd` (line 1888), `case AttachWallTopToRoofCmd` (line 1943), `case CreateRoofOpeningCmd` (line 1999), `case CreateDormerCmd` (line 2202); `apply_inplace` (line 886); `try_commit_bundle` (line 4765)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py` — all helpers: `RoofGeometryMode`, `gable_ridge_segment_plan_mm`, `gable_rectangle_fascia_edge_plan_token_v0`, convexity + L-shape predicates
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `roof` TS type (line 603), `sweep` TS type (line 1128)

## WP-011 verbatim from tracker (§6 T1 KRN-V3-03)

**KRN-V3-03 — Multi-roof composition + fascia/gutter + soffit (bundled)**

_Source: R-D §3 G11 + G12 + G13. **Bundled triple — they ship together as the "looks finished" gate.** Without all three, presentation drawings of pitched-roof houses look unfinished._

**Status.** `now`.
**Scope.** Three coupled WPs that ship as one bundle because together they are the "B1 looks-finished" gate. **(a)** Multi-roof composition via `RoofJoin` operator: pick two roofs → resolve intersection into a watertight composite (CSG-stitched). Most ≥3-bedroom houses are gable+hip+shed combinations. **(b)** Fascia / gutter / downpipe: roof-attached profile runs along eave / rake edges. Generalises into an `EdgeProfileRun` pattern that also serves G2 wall sweeps (KRN-V3-08). **(c)** Soffit: horizontal under-side of a roof eave, sketched 2D boundary + thickness, lives between roof and wall top.

**Data model.**
```ts
// (a) RoofJoin
{
  kind: 'roof_join';
  primaryRoofId: string;
  secondaryRoofId: string;
  seamMode: 'clip_secondary_into_primary' | 'merge_at_ridge';
}

// (b) EdgeProfileRun
{
  kind: 'edge_profile_run';
  hostElementId: string;            // roof | wall | floor
  hostEdge: 'eave' | 'rake' | 'ridge' | 'top' | 'bottom' | { startMm; endMm };
  profileFamilyId: string;          // 2D profile (cornice, fascia, gutter, plinth, water-table)
  offsetMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number };
  miterMode: 'auto' | 'manual';
}

// (c) Soffit
{
  kind: 'soffit';
  boundary: Array<{ xMm: number; yMm: number }>;   // 2D polygon in plan
  hostRoofId?: string;
  thicknessMm: number;
  zMm: number;
}
```
**Engine.** **(a)** `RoofJoin` apply runs Three.js / OpenCASCADE-style CSG against the two roof solids; result is a derived `roof_join` element that renders the seam. Validation: roofs must intersect; no degenerate seams. UX: select two → "Join Roofs" → show seam in `--draft-warning` orange before commit (U3 CSG-preview pattern). **(b)** `EdgeProfileRun` solves a swept solid: 2D profile × edge polyline → 3D solid. Auto-mitres at corners (≤ tolerance). Renders in plan as a thin line, in elevation / 3D as the swept solid. **(c)** Soffit is solved as a thickened 2D extrusion; snap-to-eave-edge helper places it correctly under the roof overhang.

**UI.** New tools in the right rail: "Join Roofs" (prompts for two roofs); "Edge Profile" (prompts for host edge + profile family — fascia, gutter, downpipe, plinth, cornice, water-table all ship as profile families); "Soffit" (sketch-mode polygon + thickness). The pre-commit CSG preview is the marquee UX moment.

**Acceptance.** Demo seed has a gable over the main mass + a hip over an L-extension. Pre-T1: the two roofs collide visibly at the join. Post-T1: select both → "Join Roofs" → preview seam in orange → commit; the join reads watertight from any 3D angle. Then: select the eave edge → "Edge Profile" → fascia → run in 60 fps. Then: sketch a soffit polygon under the eave → "Soffit" 50 mm thick → ships. Final demo angle: warm-shadow render of the full house with no naked edges anywhere.

**Effort.** XL — 4 weeks (1.5 weeks G11 + 1 week G12 + 0.5 week G13 + 1 week shared CSG / preview / mitering plumbing).

**Cross-theme references.** T2 (CSG-preview pattern is reusable for floor/wall joins; U3 lives here); T5 (profile families catalog: fascia, gutter, downpipe, plinth, cornice, water-table); T4 (multi-roof composition is what makes 3D presentation drawings publishable).

## Setup

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof
```

## Implementation tasks

### Part A — RoofJoin (G11, 1.5 weeks)

1. **Add `RoofJoinElem` to `elements.py`.**
   - `kind: Literal["roof_join"]`, fields: `primary_roof_id: str`, `secondary_roof_id: str`, `seam_mode: Literal["clip_secondary_into_primary", "merge_at_ridge"]`.
   - Add `"roof_join"` to the `ElementKind` literal and the `Element` discriminated union (follow the pattern of `DormerElem`).
   - Add `pinned: bool = False` and `phase_created / phase_demolished` stubs for IFC parity.

2. **Add `CreateRoofJoinCmd` to `commands.py`.**
   - Fields: `type: Literal["createRoofJoin"]`, `id: str | None = None`, `primary_roof_id: str`, `secondary_roof_id: str`, `seam_mode`.
   - Import from `elements.py` as needed. Register the command in the `Command` union at the bottom of the file.

3. **Dispatch `case CreateRoofJoinCmd()` in `engine.py`** (insert after `case CreateRoofOpeningCmd()` around line 1999).
   - Validate: both IDs resolve to `RoofElem`; primary ≠ secondary; footprints overlap (compute AABB intersection — non-empty is sufficient for MVP).
   - On degenerate / non-intersecting footprints raise `ValueError("createRoofJoin: roof footprints do not intersect")`.
   - Emit a `RoofJoinElem`; do NOT mutate the source `RoofElem` records — the join is a derived overlay element.

4. **CSG preview in the renderer** (Three.js side).
   - When a `roof_join` element is in `PENDING` state (pre-commit dry-run), render the seam polyline in `--draft-warning` CSS token colour (do not hardcode hex).
   - Post-commit: render the seam as a thin ridge line using the primary roof's `materialKey`.

5. **Add `RoofJoinElem` to the TS union in `packages/core/src/index.ts`** following the same JSDoc + field pattern as the `roof_opening` entry (line 672).

### Part B — EdgeProfileRun (G12, 1 week)

6. **Add `EdgeProfileRunElem` to `elements.py`.**
   - Fields: `kind: Literal["edge_profile_run"]`, `host_element_id: str`, `host_edge` (use a `Union[Literal["eave","rake","ridge","top","bottom"], dict]` typed as `Any` for the custom `{startMm, endMm}` variant), `profile_family_id: str`, `offset_mm: Vec2Mm`, `miter_mode: Literal["auto", "manual"]`.
   - Add to `ElementKind` and `Element` union.

7. **Add `CreateEdgeProfileRunCmd` to `commands.py`.** Mirror the field layout from `EdgeProfileRunElem`. Register in the `Command` union.

8. **Dispatch `case CreateEdgeProfileRunCmd()` in `engine.py`.**
   - Validate: `hostElementId` resolves; `profileFamilyId` is a non-empty string (catalog lookup deferred to T5 — accept any non-empty string for now); `miterMode` is one of the two literals.
   - Resolve the host edge polyline: for `RoofElem` + `hostEdge="eave"`, use `gable_rectangle_fascia_edge_plan_token_v0` from `roof_geometry.py` to derive the eave edge pairs; for `hostEdge="rake"`, use the corresponding gable rake edges.
   - Emit an `EdgeProfileRunElem`.

9. **Swept-solid geometry helper in `roof_geometry.py`.**
   - Add `edge_profile_run_path_mm(roof: RoofElem, host_edge: str) -> list[tuple[float, float, float]]` — returns 3D waypoints for the profile path from roof geometry. Keep it pure (no element dict lookups — caller passes the resolved `RoofElem`).
   - Auto-mitre: at each interior corner of the path, compute the bisector angle and set the miter trim length.

10. **TS type** for `edge_profile_run` in `packages/core/src/index.ts`.

### Part C — Soffit (G13, 0.5 weeks)

11. **Add `SoffitElem` to `elements.py`.**
    - Fields: `kind: Literal["soffit"]`, `boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm]`, `host_roof_id: str | None`, `thickness_mm: float`, `z_mm: float`.
    - `boundary_mm` must have ≥ 3 vertices (add `model_validator`).
    - Add to `ElementKind` and `Element` union.

12. **Add `CreateSoffitCmd` to `commands.py`.**

13. **Dispatch `case CreateSoffitCmd()` in `engine.py`.**
    - Validate ≥ 3 boundary vertices; `thicknessMm > 0`; if `hostRoofId` is set, it must resolve to a `RoofElem`.
    - Snap-to-eave helper: if `hostRoofId` is provided and `zMm` is not supplied, compute `z_mm` as the eave elevation derived from the host roof's `referenceLevelId` elevation (look up in `els`).
    - Emit a `SoffitElem`.

14. **TS type** for `soffit` in `packages/core/src/index.ts`.

### Shared / plumbing (1 week)

15. **Write `app/tests/test_roof_join.py`** — ≥ 8 tests:
    - Happy path: two intersecting gable roofs → `roof_join` emitted.
    - Rejects identical `primaryRoofId` == `secondaryRoofId`.
    - Rejects non-intersecting footprints (AABB no-overlap).
    - `seam_mode` round-trips through `RoofJoinElem`.

16. **Write `app/tests/test_edge_profile_run.py`** — ≥ 6 tests:
    - Happy path: fascia on eave edge of a gable roof.
    - Rejects unknown `hostElementId`.
    - Rejects empty `profileFamilyId`.
    - `auto` vs `manual` miterMode stored correctly.

17. **Write `app/tests/test_soffit.py`** — ≥ 6 tests:
    - Happy path with explicit `zMm`.
    - Snap-to-eave: `zMm` derived from host roof when omitted.
    - Rejects < 3 boundary vertices.
    - Rejects `thicknessMm <= 0`.

## Implementation rules

- **API-V3-01 contract:** every new command must also register a `ToolDescriptor` in `app/bim_ai/api/` if that registry already exists; if it does not exist yet, add a `# TODO API-V3-01` comment stub next to each new `case` block in `engine.py`.
- **No hex literals in chrome:** the CSG-preview seam colour must use `var(--draft-warning)` CSS token — never a hardcoded `#` value. Profile run and soffit geometry colours must likewise use material tokens from T5, not inline hex.
- **Pure geometry helpers:** functions added to `roof_geometry.py` must remain pure (no side effects, no `els` dict access). Pass resolved elements as arguments.
- **ElementKind exhaustiveness:** after adding the three new `kind` literals, run `python -c "from bim_ai.elements import Element"` — any missing discriminator will raise immediately.
- **Do not widen `RoofElem`:** these three elements are _separate_ from `RoofElem`. Never add `roofJoinId`, `soffitIds`, etc. to `RoofElem`; use the host-reference pattern (`hostRoofId`) instead.
- **Seam rendering uses derived geometry only:** `RoofJoinElem` stores only references and `seamMode`; the renderer computes the seam polyline on the fly from the two `RoofElem` footprints.

## engine.py overlap warning

**WP-004 (dormer CSG) may have already merged into `main` before this branch was cut.** The dormer dispatch block (`case CreateDormerCmd()`, currently around line 2202) and the `_dormer_host_floor` helper (around line 555) both touch roof element lookups. Before adding the three new `case` blocks:

1. `git rebase origin/main` and resolve any conflicts in the roof dispatch region (lines ~1888–2250).
2. Do not disturb the `CreateDormerCmd` case — add `CreateRoofJoinCmd`, `CreateEdgeProfileRunCmd`, `CreateSoffitCmd` immediately after `CreateRoofOpeningCmd` (line ~1999), before `CreateDormerCmd`.
3. After rebasing, re-run `make verify` before writing any new code to confirm the baseline is clean.

## Verify gate

```bash
# Python tests
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app
python -m pytest tests/test_roof_join.py tests/test_edge_profile_run.py tests/test_soffit.py -v

# Full suite must stay green
make verify

# TypeScript must type-check cleanly
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
pnpm tsc --noEmit

# ElementKind smoke-check
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app
python -c "from bim_ai.elements import Element; print('ElementKind OK')"
```

## Commit and push

```bash
git add app/bim_ai/elements.py \
        app/bim_ai/commands.py \
        app/bim_ai/engine.py \
        app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py \
        app/tests/test_roof_join.py \
        app/tests/test_edge_profile_run.py \
        app/tests/test_soffit.py \
        packages/core/src/index.ts

git commit -m "feat(krn): KRN-V3-03 — RoofJoin + EdgeProfileRun + Soffit (G11/G12/G13)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof
```

## Final report

Paste this back to the orchestrator when done:

```

Branch: feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof
Status: done
Tests: <N> passed  (roof_join: X, edge_profile_run: X, soffit: X)
Files changed: <list key files>
Notes: <anything unexpected — seam accuracy, miter edge cases, TS union changes>
```

class StairRun(BaseModel):
    """KRN-07: one flight in a multi-run stair.

    Straight runs use start_mm/end_mm. Curved runs (spiral, sketch) populate
    polyline_mm with ≥2 plan-coordinate points; renderers read polyline_mm when
    present and fall back to start/end otherwise.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    id: str
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=1000)
    riser_count: int = Field(alias="riserCount", default=8)
    polyline_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="polylineMm")


class StairLanding(BaseModel):
    """KRN-07: a flat polygon landing between two runs."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    id: str
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")


class StairElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["stair"] = "stair"
    id: str
    name: str = "Stair"
    base_level_id: str = Field(alias="baseLevelId")
    top_level_id: str = Field(alias="topLevelId")
    run_start: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runStartMm")
    run_end: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runEndMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=1000)
    riser_mm: float = Field(alias="riserMm", default=175)
    tread_mm: float = Field(alias="treadMm", default=275)
    # KRN-07 — multi-run support. Defaults preserve the legacy single-run shape.
    shape: StairShape = Field(default="straight")
    runs: list[StairRun] = Field(default_factory=list)
    landings: list[StairLanding] = Field(default_factory=list)
    # IFC-04: optional classification code emitted as IfcClassificationReference.
    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")
    # KRN-07 closeout — spiral + sketch shape inputs.
    center_mm: Vec2Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="centerMm")
    inner_radius_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="innerRadiusMm")
    outer_radius_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="outerRadiusMm")
    total_rotation_deg: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="totalRotationDeg")
    sketch_path_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="sketchPathMm")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _validate_shape_specific_fields(self) -> StairElem:
        if self.shape == "spiral":
            missing = [
                name
                for name, value in (
                    ("centerMm", self.center_mm),
                    ("innerRadiusMm", self.inner_radius_mm),
                    ("outerRadiusMm", self.outer_radius_mm),
                    ("totalRotationDeg", self.total_rotation_deg),
                )
                if value is None
            ]
            if missing:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"spiral stair requires {', '.join(missing)}",
                )
            if (
                self.inner_radius_mm is not None
                and self.outer_radius_mm is not None
                and self.outer_radius_mm <= self.inner_radius_mm
            ):
                raise ValueError("spiral stair outerRadiusMm must exceed innerRadiusMm")
        elif self.shape == "sketch":
            if self.sketch_path_mm is None or len(self.sketch_path_mm) < 2:
                raise ValueError("sketch stair requires sketchPathMm with at least two points")
        return self


class SlabOpeningElem(BaseModel):

    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)


class RoofOpeningElem(BaseModel):
    """IFC-03: opening hosted on a roof (skylight / roof penetration).

    The opening's footprint is given in plan coordinates (x, y). The
    roof renderer CSG-subtracts a vertical extrusion of this footprint
    spanning the roof body. Validation: footprint must lie within the
    host roof's plan footprint.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["roof_opening"] = "roof_opening"
    id: str
    name: str = "Roof opening"
    host_roof_id: str = Field(alias="hostRoofId")
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)

class DormerElem(BaseModel):
    """KRN-14 — dormer cut through host roof + dormer walls + roof."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["dormer"] = "dormer"
    id: str
    name: str = "Dormer"
    host_roof_id: str = Field(alias="hostRoofId")
    position_on_roof: DormerPositionOnRoof = Field(alias="positionOnRoof")
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", gt=0)
    wall_height_mm: float = Field(alias="wallHeightMm", gt=0)
    depth_mm: float = Field(alias="depthMm", gt=0)
    dormer_roof_kind: DormerRoofKind = Field(default="flat", alias="dormerRoofKind")
    dormer_roof_pitch_deg: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="dormerRoofPitchDeg")
    ridge_height_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="ridgeHeightMm")
    wall_material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="wallMaterialKey")
    roof_material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="roofMaterialKey")
    has_floor_opening: bool = Field(default=False, alias="hasFloorOpening")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _ridge_height_required_for_pitched(self) -> DormerElem:
        if self.dormer_roof_kind in ("gable", "hipped"):
            if self.ridge_height_mm is None or self.ridge_height_mm <= 0:
                raise ValueError(
                    "DormerElem.ridgeHeightMm must be > 0 when dormerRoofKind is "
                    "'gable' or 'hipped'"
                )
        return self

    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")

class CreateStairCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createStair"] = "createStair"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Stair"
    base_level_id: str = Field(alias="baseLevelId")
    top_level_id: str = Field(alias="topLevelId")
    run_start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runStartMm")
    run_end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runEndMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=1000)
    riser_mm: float = Field(alias="riserMm", default=175)
    tread_mm: float = Field(alias="treadMm", default=275)
    # KRN-07 — multi-run support. Defaults preserve legacy single-run behavior.
    shape: Literal["straight", "l_shape", "u_shape", "spiral", "sketch"] = Field(
        default="straight"
    )
    runs: list[StairRun] = Field(default_factory=list)
    landings: list[StairLanding] = Field(default_factory=list)
    # KRN-07 closeout — spiral + sketch shape inputs.
    center_mm: Vec2Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="centerMm")
    inner_radius_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="innerRadiusMm")
    outer_radius_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="outerRadiusMm")
    total_rotation_deg: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="totalRotationDeg")
    riser_count: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="riserCount")
    sketch_path_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="sketchPathMm")

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _validate_shape_specific_fields(self) -> CreateStairCmd:
        if self.shape == "spiral":
            missing: list[str] = []
            if self.center_mm is None:
                missing.append("centerMm")
            if self.inner_radius_mm is None:
                missing.append("innerRadiusMm")
            if self.outer_radius_mm is None:
                missing.append("outerRadiusMm")
            if self.total_rotation_deg is None:
                missing.append("totalRotationDeg")
            if self.riser_count is None or self.riser_count < 1:
                missing.append("riserCount")
            if missing:
                raise ValueError(f"spiral stair requires {', '.join(missing)}")
            if (
                self.inner_radius_mm is not None
                and self.outer_radius_mm is not None
                and self.outer_radius_mm <= self.inner_radius_mm
            ):
                raise ValueError("spiral stair outerRadiusMm must exceed innerRadiusMm")
        elif self.shape == "sketch":
            if self.sketch_path_mm is None or len(self.sketch_path_mm) < 2:
                raise ValueError("sketch stair requires sketchPathMm with at least two points")
        return self

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createBalcony"] = "createBalcony"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Balcony"
    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
    elevation_mm: float = Field(alias="elevationMm")
    projection_mm: float = Field(default=650, alias="projectionMm")
    slab_thickness_mm: float = Field(default=150, alias="slabThicknessMm")
    balustrade_height_mm: float = Field(default=1050, alias="balustradeHeightMm")


class CreateRailingCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createRailing"] = "createRailing"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Railing"
    hosted_stair_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hostedStairId")
    path_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="pathMm")


class FamilyCatalogSourceCmd(BaseModel):
    """FAM-08 — provenance triple stored on a family_type loaded from a catalog."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    catalog_id: str = Field(alias="catalogId")
    family_id: str = Field(alias="familyId")
    version: str


class UpsertFamilyTypeCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["upsertFamilyType"] = "upsertFamilyType"
    id: str | None = None
    discipline: Literal["door", "window", "generic"] = "generic"
    parameters: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
    catalog_source: FamilyCatalogSourceCmd | None = Field(
        default=None, alias="catalogSource"
    )


class AssignOpeningFamilyCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["assignOpeningFamily"] = "assignOpeningFamily"
    opening_id: str = Field(alias="openingId")
    family_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="familyTypeId")
    cut_depth_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="cutDepthMm")
    reveal_interior_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="revealInteriorMm")


class UpdateOpeningCleanroomCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["updateOpeningCleanroom"] = "updateOpeningCleanroom"
    opening_id: str = Field(alias="openingId")
    interlock_grade: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="interlockGrade")
    seal_rebate_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="sealRebateMm")
    lod_plan: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="lodPlan")

1	"""Gable roof footprint math shared by glTF export (WP-B04 / WP-X02)."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import math
6	from typing import Literal
7	
8	RoofGeometryMode = Literal[
9	    "mass_box",
10	    "gable_pitched_rectangle",
11	    "asymmetric_gable",
12	    "gable_pitched_l_shape",
13	    "hip",
14	    "flat",
15	]
16	
17	RoofGeometrySupportTokenV0 = Literal[
18	    "gable_pitched_rectangle_supported",
19	    "gable_pitched_l_shape_supported",
20	    "hip_supported",
21	    "hip_candidate_deferred",
22	    "valley_candidate_deferred",
23	    "non_rectangular_footprint_deferred",
24	    "missing_slope_or_level",
25	]
26	
27	RoofPlanGeometryReadoutV0 = Literal[
28	    "gable_projection_supported",
29	    "mass_box_peak_proxy",
30	    "footprint_proxy_deferred",
31	]
32	
33	FootprintPlanWinding = Literal["ccw", "cw", "degenerate"]
34	RidgeAxisPlan = Literal["alongX", "alongZ"]
35

37	def outer_rect_extent(pts: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> tuple[float, float, float, float]:
38	    """Axis-aligned bounds on the plan footprint; maps to world X and Z."""
39	    xs = [p[0] for p in pts]
40	    zs = [p[1] for p in pts]
41	    return float(min(xs)), float(max(xs)), float(min(zs)), float(max(zs))
42	
43	
44	def gable_half_run_mm_and_ridge_axis(span_x: float, span_z: float) -> tuple[float, str]:
45	    """Ridge parallels the shorter footprint span; slope half-run is half the longer span (mm)."""
46	    if span_x <= span_z:
47	        return span_z / 2.0, "alongX"
48	    return span_x / 2.0, "alongZ"
49	
50	
51	def gable_ridge_rise_mm(span_x: float, span_z: float, slope_deg: float) -> tuple[float, str]:
52	    half_run, axis = gable_half_run_mm_and_ridge_axis(span_x, span_z)
53	    rise_mm = half_run * math.tan(math.radians(slope_deg))
54	    return rise_mm, axis
55	
56	
57	def plan_polygon_signed_area_mm2(pts: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> float:
58	    """Signed shoelace area on plan coordinates (x, z aliases stored as xMm/yMm)."""
59	
60	    n = len(pts)
61	    if n < 3:
62	        return 0.0
63	    s = 0.0
64	    for i in range(n):
65	        x1, z1 = pts[i]
66	        x2, z2 = pts[(i + 1) % n]
67	        s += x1 * z2 - x2 * z1
68	    return s * 0.5
69	
70	
71	def plan_polygon_winding_token(area_mm2: float, *, eps_mm2: float = 1.0) -> FootprintPlanWinding:
72	    if abs(area_mm2) <= eps_mm2:
73	        return "degenerate"
74	    return "ccw" if area_mm2 > 0 else "cw"
75	
76	
77	def plan_simple_polygon_is_convex_mm(
78	    pts: list[tuple[float, float]],
79	    *,
80	    cross_eps: float = 1e-6,
81	) -> bool:
82	    """Plan convexity for a simple closed ring (x/z); collinear segments are ignored for sign."""
83	
84	    n = len(pts)
85	    if n < 3:
86	        return False
87	    signs: list[int] = []
88	    for i in range(n):
89	        x0, z0 = pts[i]
90	        x1, z1 = pts[(i + 1) % n]
91	        x2, z2 = pts[(i + 2) % n]
92	        v1x, v1z = x1 - x0, z1 - z0
93	        v2x, v2z = x2 - x1, z2 - z1
94	        cross = v1x * v2z - v1z * v2x
95	        if abs(cross) <= cross_eps:
96	            continue
97	        signs.append(1 if cross > 0 else -1)
98	    if not signs:
99	        return False
100	    return len(set(signs)) <= 1
101	
102	
103	def plan_simple_polygon_is_concave_mm(
104	    pts: list[tuple[float, float]],
105	    *,
106	    area_eps_mm2: float = 1.0,
107	    cross_eps: float = 1e-6,
108	) -> bool:
109	    """True when footprint has an interior reflex corner (e.g. L-shape)."""
110	
111	    if len(pts) < 3:
112	        return False
113	    area = plan_polygon_signed_area_mm2(pts)
114	    if plan_polygon_winding_token(area, eps_mm2=area_eps_mm2) == "degenerate":
115	        return False
116	    return not plan_simple_polygon_is_convex_mm(pts, cross_eps=cross_eps)
117	
118	
119	def gable_pitched_rectangle_elevation_supported_v0(
120	    *,
121	    footprint_mm: list[tuple[float, float]],
122	    roof_geometry_mode: RoofGeometryMode,
123	    reference_level_resolves: bool,
124	    slope_deg: float | None,
125	) -> bool:
126	    """True when gable ridge / gable mesh is representative (rectangle + mode + level + slope)."""
127	
128	    return (
129	        roof_geometry_support_token_v0(
130	            footprint_mm=footprint_mm,
131	            roof_geometry_mode=roof_geometry_mode,
132	            reference_level_resolves=reference_level_resolves,
133	            slope_deg=slope_deg,
134	        )
135	        == "gable_pitched_rectangle_supported"
136	    )
137	
138	
139	def roof_plan_geometry_readout_v0(
140	    *,
141	    roof_geometry_mode: RoofGeometryMode,
142	    roof_geometry_support_token: RoofGeometrySupportTokenV0 | None,
143	    gable_elevation_supported: bool,
144	) -> RoofPlanGeometryReadoutV0:
145	    """Compact agent-facing token: full gable chord vs mass-box peak proxy vs deferred footprint."""
146	
147	    if gable_elevation_supported:
148	        return "gable_projection_supported"
149	    if roof_geometry_mode == "mass_box" and roof_geometry_support_token is None:
150	        return "mass_box_peak_proxy"
151	    return "footprint_proxy_deferred"
152	
153	
154	def roof_geometry_support_token_v0(
155	    *,
156	    footprint_mm: list[tuple[float, float]],
157	    roof_geometry_mode: RoofGeometryMode,
158	    reference_level_resolves: bool,
159	    slope_deg: float | None,
160	) -> RoofGeometrySupportTokenV0 | None:
161	    """Deterministic hip/valley/skip matrix; None for ordinary mass_box axis-aligned rectangles."""
162	
163	    if not reference_level_resolves or len(footprint_mm) < 3 or slope_deg is None:
164	        return "missing_slope_or_level"
165	
166	    area = plan_polygon_signed_area_mm2(footprint_mm)
167	    if plan_polygon_winding_token(area) == "degenerate":
168	        return "non_rectangular_footprint_deferred"
169	
170	    if plan_simple_polygon_is_concave_mm(footprint_mm):
171	        if roof_geometry_mode == "gable_pitched_l_shape" and footprint_is_valid_l_shape_mm(
172	            footprint_mm
173	        ):
174	            return "gable_pitched_l_shape_supported"
175	        return "valley_candidate_deferred"
176	
177	    if roof_geometry_mode in (
178	        "gable_pitched_rectangle",
179	        "asymmetric_gable",
180	    ) and footprint_is_valid_axis_aligned_rectangle_mm(footprint_mm):
181	        return "gable_pitched_rectangle_supported"
182	
183	    is_convex = plan_simple_polygon_is_convex_mm(footprint_mm)
184	    is_rect = footprint_is_valid_axis_aligned_rectangle_mm(footprint_mm)
185	    if roof_geometry_mode == "hip" and is_convex and len(footprint_mm) >= 4:
186	        return "hip_supported"
187	    if is_convex and len(footprint_mm) >= 4 and not is_rect:
188	        return "hip_candidate_deferred"
189	
190	    if roof_geometry_mode == "mass_box" and is_rect and is_convex:
191	        return None
192	
193	    return "non_rectangular_footprint_deferred"
194	
195	
196	def footprint_is_valid_axis_aligned_rectangle_mm(footprint_mm: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> bool:
197	    """Same geometric predicate as assert_valid_gable_pitched_rectangle_footprint_mm, non-throwing."""
198	
199	    if len(footprint_mm) != 4:
200	        return False
201	    xs = [p[0] for p in footprint_mm]
202	    zs = [p[1] for p in footprint_mm]
203	    x0, x1 = min(xs), max(xs)
204	    z0, z1 = min(zs), max(zs)
205	    tol = 1.0
206	    corners = {(x0, z0), (x0, z1), (x1, z0), (x1, z1)}
207	    hit: set[tuple[float, float]] = set()
208	    for p in footprint_mm:
209	        matched = False
210	        for c in corners:
211	            if abs(p[0] - c[0]) <= tol and abs(p[1] - c[1]) <= tol:
212	                hit.add(c)
213	                matched = True
214	                break
215	        if not matched:
216	            return False
217	    return len(hit) == 4
218	
219	
220	def gable_ridge_segment_plan_mm(
221	    x0_mm: float,
222	    x1_mm: float,
223	    z0_mm: float,
224	    z1_mm: float,
225	    ridge_axis: RidgeAxisPlan,
226	) -> tuple[tuple[float, float], tuple[float, float]]:
227	    """Ridge centerline in plan mm between footprint extents (parallel to ridge_axis)."""
228	
229	    if ridge_axis == "alongX":
230	        zm = (z0_mm + z1_mm) * 0.5
231	        return (x0_mm, zm), (x1_mm, zm)
232	    xm = (x0_mm + x1_mm) * 0.5
233	    return (xm, z0_mm), (xm, z1_mm)
234	
235	
236	def gable_rectangle_fascia_edge_plan_token_v0(ridge_axis: RidgeAxisPlan) -> str:
237	    """Deterministic plan-axis roles for rectangle gable roofs (eaves parallel to ridge; rake at gable ends)."""
238	
239	    if ridge_axis == "alongX":
240	        return "eaveParallelPlanX_gableRakeParallelPlanZ"
241	    return "eaveParallelPlanZ_gableRakeParallelPlanX"
242	
243	
244	def mass_box_roof_proxy_peak_z_mm(
245	    reference_level_elevation_mm: float, slope_deg: float | None
246	) -> float:
247	    """Section/plan proxy peak for roofGeometryMode=mass_box (800 mm nominal half-run heuristic)."""
248	
249	    slope = float(slope_deg if slope_deg is not None else 25.0)
250	    rise = 800.0 * math.tan(math.radians(slope))
251	    return float(reference_level_elevation_mm) + rise
252	
253	
254	def assert_valid_gable_pitched_rectangle_footprint_mm(
255	    footprint_mm: list[tuple[float, float]],
256	) -> None:
257	    """Require four axis-aligned rectangle corners (plan mm)."""
258	    if len(footprint_mm) != 4:
259	        raise ValueError(
260	            "gable_pitched_rectangle footprintMm must be exactly 4 vertices (axis-aligned rectangle)"
261	        )
262	    if not footprint_is_valid_axis_aligned_rectangle_mm(footprint_mm):
263	        raise ValueError(
264	            "gable_pitched_rectangle footprintMm must be an axis-aligned rectangle (corners only)"
265	        )
266	
267	
268	def footprint_is_valid_l_shape_mm(footprint_mm: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> bool:
269	    """KRN-02: True for axis-aligned L-shape — six vertices, exactly one reflex corner."""
270	
271	    if len(footprint_mm) != 6:
272	        return False
273	    area = plan_polygon_signed_area_mm2(footprint_mm)
274	    if plan_polygon_winding_token(area) == "degenerate":
275	        return False
276	    n = len(footprint_mm)
277	    wsign = 1 if area > 0 else -1
278	    reflex_count = 0
279	    for i in range(n):
280	        a = footprint_mm[(i - 1) % n]
281	        b = footprint_mm[i]
282	        c = footprint_mm[(i + 1) % n]
283	        cross = (b[0] - a[0]) * (c[1] - b[1]) - (b[1] - a[1]) * (c[0] - b[0])
284	        if cross * wsign < -1e-6:
285	            reflex_count += 1
286	    if reflex_count != 1:
287	        return False
288	    # All edges must be axis-aligned for the L-shape gable to render correctly.
289	    tol = 1.0
290	    for i in range(n):
291	        a = footprint_mm[i]
292	        b = footprint_mm[(i + 1) % n]
293	        if abs(a[0] - b[0]) > tol and abs(a[1] - b[1]) > tol:
294	            return False
295	    return True
296	
297	
298	def assert_valid_l_shape_footprint_mm(footprint_mm: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> None:
299	    """KRN-02: gable_pitched_l_shape requires axis-aligned 6-vertex L footprint."""
300	
301	    if not footprint_is_valid_l_shape_mm(footprint_mm):
302	        raise ValueError(
303	            "gable_pitched_l_shape footprintMm must be an axis-aligned 6-vertex L-shape"
304	            " with exactly one reflex corner"
305	        )
306	
307	
308	def assert_valid_hip_footprint_mm(footprint_mm: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> None:
309	    """KRN-03: hip mode requires a convex polygon with ≥ 4 vertices."""
310	
311	    if len(footprint_mm) < 4:
312	        raise ValueError("hip footprintMm requires at least 4 vertices (convex polygon)")
313	    if not plan_simple_polygon_is_convex_mm(footprint_mm):
314	        raise ValueError("hip footprintMm must be a convex polygon")

        case CreateSlabOpeningCmd():

                font_size_mm=cmd.font_size_mm,
                depth_mm=cmd.depth_mm,
                position_mm=cmd.position_mm,
                rotation_deg=cmd.rotation_deg,
                material_key=cmd.material_key,
            )

        case CreateWallOpeningCmd():
            oid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if oid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{oid}'")
            host = els.get(cmd.host_wall_id)
            if not isinstance(host, WallElem):
                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.hostWallId must reference a wall")
            if cmd.along_t_start >= cmd.along_t_end:
                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.alongTStart must be < alongTEnd")
            if cmd.head_height_mm <= cmd.sill_height_mm:
                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.headHeightMm must be > sillHeightMm")
            if cmd.head_height_mm > host.height_mm:
                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.headHeightMm must not exceed host wall height")
            els[oid] = WallOpeningElem(
                kind="wall_opening",
                id=oid,
                name=cmd.name,
                host_wall_id=cmd.host_wall_id,
                along_t_start=cmd.along_t_start,
                along_t_end=cmd.along_t_end,
                sill_height_mm=cmd.sill_height_mm,

class ColumnElem(BaseModel):

    Cross-section is a rectangle (bMm × hMm) with optional rotation about
    the vertical axis. Spans from the host level upward by ``heightMm``;
    optional top constraint mirrors the wall datum-constraint pattern.

    name: str = "Column"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    b_mm: float = Field(alias="bMm", default=300, gt=0)
    h_mm: float = Field(alias="hMm", default=300, gt=0)
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=2800, gt=0)
    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    base_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="baseConstraintOffsetMm")
    top_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="topConstraintLevelId")
    top_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="topConstraintOffsetMm")
    # IFC-04: optional classification code emitted as IfcClassificationReference.
    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")

    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, model_validator

from bim_ai.elements import (
    CameraMm,
    ConstraintRefRow,
    ConstraintRule,
    CurtainPanelOverride,
    DormerPositionOnRoof,
    DormerRoofKind,
    DxfLineworkPrim,
    EvidenceRef,
    PhaseFilter,
    PlanCategoryGraphicRow,
    PlanTagBadgeStyle,
    PlanTagTarget,
    RoomColorSchemeRow,
    SiteContextObjectRow,
    StairLanding,
    StairRun,
    SweepPathPoint,
    SweepProfilePlane,
    SweepProfilePoint,
    Text3dFontFamily,
    Vec2Mm,
    Vec3Mm,
    WallRecessZone,
    WallTypeLayer,
)
from bim_ai.roof_geometry import RoofGeometryMode


class CreateLevelCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createLevel"] = "createLevel"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Level"
    elevation_mm: float = Field(alias="elevationMm", default=0)
    datum_kind: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="datumKind")
    parent_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="parentLevelId")
    offset_from_parent_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="offsetFromParentMm")
    # VIE-05: when True (default), the engine also creates a "<name> — Plan"
    # plan_view referencing the new level so the common flow needs no follow-up.
    also_create_plan_view: bool = Field(default=True, alias="alsoCreatePlanView")
    plan_view_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="planViewId")

class ReconcileMonitoredElementCmd(BaseModel):
    """FED-03: resolve drift on a single monitored element.

    ``mode`` is either ``'accept_source'`` (overwrite host fields with the
    current source values; clears ``drifted``) or ``'keep_host'`` (bump
    ``source_revision_at_copy`` to the source's current revision and clear
    ``drifted`` without touching host fields).
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["reconcileMonitoredElement"] = "reconcileMonitoredElement"
    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
    mode: Literal["accept_source", "keep_host"]

# ---- EDT-04: Plan-canvas Modify tools (Split / Align / Trim / Wall-Join) ----


class SplitWallAtCmd(BaseModel):
    """EDT-04 — split a wall at a normalised position alongT into two walls.

    The original wall is replaced by two new walls that share the split
    point. Hosted openings are *not* migrated by this command (the canvas
    side of the SD tool stays out of opening reassignment for v1); the
    along-T parameter and any door/window remain anchored to whichever
    of the two resulting walls now hosts the opening.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["splitWallAt"] = "splitWallAt"
    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
    along_t: float = Field(alias="alongT", gt=0, lt=1)


class AlignElementToReferenceCmd(BaseModel):
    """EDT-04 — translate a target wall so its near endpoint snaps to the
    reference point along the closer principal axis."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["alignElementToReference"] = "alignElementToReference"
    target_wall_id: str = Field(alias="targetWallId")
    reference_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="referenceMm")


class TrimElementToReferenceCmd(BaseModel):
    """EDT-04 — extend or trim ``targetWallId`` so its ``endHint`` endpoint
    lies on the infinite line of ``referenceWallId``."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["trimElementToReference"] = "trimElementToReference"
    reference_wall_id: str = Field(alias="referenceWallId")
    target_wall_id: str = Field(alias="targetWallId")
    end_hint: Literal["start", "end"] = Field(alias="endHint")


WallJoinVariant = Literal["miter", "butt", "square"]


class SetWallJoinVariantCmd(BaseModel):
    """EDT-04 — record the join variant for the walls meeting at a corner."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["setWallJoinVariant"] = "setWallJoinVariant"
    wall_ids: list[str] = Field(alias="wallIds")
    variant: WallJoinVariant


# ---- EDT-04: Single-/two-click placement create commands ----


class CreateColumnCmd(BaseModel):
    """EDT-04 — single-click structural-column placement."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createColumn"] = "createColumn"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Column"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    b_mm: float = Field(alias="bMm", default=300, gt=0)
    h_mm: float = Field(alias="hMm", default=300, gt=0)
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=2800, gt=0)
    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")


class CreateBeamCmd(BaseModel):
    """EDT-04 — two-click structural-beam placement."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createBeam"] = "createBeam"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Beam"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=200, gt=0)
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=400, gt=0)
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")


class CreateCeilingCmd(BaseModel):
    """EDT-04 — sketch-polygon ceiling placement on a level."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createCeiling"] = "createCeiling"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Ceiling"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    height_offset_mm: float = Field(alias="heightOffsetMm", default=2700)
    thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="thicknessMm", default=20, gt=0)
    ceiling_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ceilingTypeId")


class CreateMassCmd(BaseModel):
    """SKT-01 — in-place generic mass authored from a sketch session."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createMass"] = "createMass"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Mass"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    footprint_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="footprintMm")
    height_mm: float = Field(default=3000, alias="heightMm", gt=0)
    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")


class MaterializeMassToWallsCmd(BaseModel):
    """SKB-02 — auto-extract walls + floor + roof-stub from a `mass` element.

    The engine emits one wall per footprint segment, one floor matching the
    footprint at level base, and one flat roof at level base + heightMm,
    promotes phase to ``'skeleton'`` on emitted elements, and deletes the
    source mass. Each emitted element carries an ``AgentDeviationElem`` back
    to the source mass id so the materialise step is auditable.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["materializeMassToWalls"] = "materializeMassToWalls"
    mass_id: str = Field(alias="massId")


class CreateVoidCutCmd(BaseModel):
    """SKT-01 — subtractive boolean marker against a host element.

    The element is a marker only (`VoidCutElem`); the actual CSG geometry is
    handled at render time. An `AgentDeviationElem` is co-authored by the
    engine handler so the deviation against the host is traceable.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createVoidCut"] = "createVoidCut"
    id: str | None = None
    host_element_id: str = Field(alias="hostElementId")
    profile_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="profileMm")
    depth_mm: float = Field(alias="depthMm", gt=0)


class CreateConstraintCmd(BaseModel):
    """EDT-02 — author a geometric constraint between element groups.

    The padlock UI on a temp-dimension authors `equal_distance` between
    two walls; other rules are accepted shapes for forward compatibility
    but currently pass-through in the evaluator.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createConstraint"] = "createConstraint"
    id: str | None = None

class VoidCutElem(BaseModel):
    """SKT-01 — subtractive-boolean marker against a host element.

    The geometry is a closed profile + extrusion depth; the renderer is
    responsible for performing the actual CSG. The element exists in the
    document so it survives undo / redo and IFC export.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["void_cut"] = "void_cut"
    id: str
    host_element_id: str = Field(alias="hostElementId")
    profile_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="profileMm")
    depth_mm: float = Field(alias="depthMm", gt=0)


class PhaseElem(BaseModel):
    """KRN-V3-01 — project-level phasing primitive.

    Default chain: Existing (ord=0) → Demolition (ord=1) → New (ord=2).
    ``ord`` governs display order and element classification: phase_created.ord ==
    view.phase.ord → new; < view.phase.ord → existing or demolition.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["phase"] = "phase"
    id: str
    name: str
    ord: int = 0

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, field_validator, model_validator

from bim_ai.roof_geometry import RoofGeometryMode

# --- Geometry primitives ------------------------------------------------------------


class Vec2Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")


class Vec3Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")


class CameraMm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    position: Vec3Mm
    target: Vec3Mm
    up: Vec3Mm

2044	        case CreateText3dCmd():
2045	            tid = cmd.id or new_id()
2046	            if tid in els:
2047	                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{tid}'")
2048	            if not cmd.text:
2049	                raise ValueError("createText3d.text must be a non-empty string")
2050	            els[tid] = Text3dElem(
2051	                kind="text_3d",
2052	                id=tid,
2053	                text=cmd.text,
2054	                font_family=cmd.font_family,
2055	                font_size_mm=cmd.font_size_mm,
2056	                depth_mm=cmd.depth_mm,
2057	                position_mm=cmd.position_mm,
2058	                rotation_deg=cmd.rotation_deg,
2059	                material_key=cmd.material_key,
2060	            )
2061	
2062	        case CreateWallOpeningCmd():
2063	            oid = cmd.id or new_id()
2064	            if oid in els:
2065	                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{oid}'")
2066	            host = els.get(cmd.host_wall_id)
2067	            if not isinstance(host, WallElem):
2068	                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.hostWallId must reference a wall")
2069	            if cmd.along_t_start >= cmd.along_t_end:
2070	                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.alongTStart must be < alongTEnd")
2071	            if cmd.head_height_mm <= cmd.sill_height_mm:
2072	                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.headHeightMm must be > sillHeightMm")
2073	            if cmd.head_height_mm > host.height_mm:
2074	                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.headHeightMm must not exceed host wall height")
2075	            els[oid] = WallOpeningElem(
2076	                kind="wall_opening",
2077	                id=oid,
2078	                name=cmd.name,
2079	                host_wall_id=cmd.host_wall_id,
2080	                along_t_start=cmd.along_t_start,
2081	                along_t_end=cmd.along_t_end,
2082	                sill_height_mm=cmd.sill_height_mm,
2083	                head_height_mm=cmd.head_height_mm,
2084	            )
2085	
2086	        case UpdateWallOpeningCmd():
2087	            wo = els.get(cmd.opening_id)
2088	            if not isinstance(wo, WallOpeningElem):
2089	                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening.openingId must reference a wall_opening")
2090	            host_wall = els.get(wo.host_wall_id)
2091	            if not isinstance(host_wall, WallElem):
2092	                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening host wall missing")
2093	            new_along_start = (
2094	                cmd.along_t_start if cmd.along_t_start is not None else wo.along_t_start
2095	            )
2096	            new_along_end = cmd.along_t_end if cmd.along_t_end is not None else wo.along_t_end
2097	            new_sill = cmd.sill_height_mm if cmd.sill_height_mm is not None else wo.sill_height_mm
2098	            new_head = cmd.head_height_mm if cmd.head_height_mm is not None else wo.head_height_mm
2099	            if new_along_start >= new_along_end:
2100	                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening alongTStart must be < alongTEnd")
2101	            if new_head <= new_sill:
2102	                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening headHeightMm must be > sillHeightMm")
2103	            if new_head > host_wall.height_mm:
2104	                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening headHeightMm must not exceed host wall height")
2105	            els[cmd.opening_id] = wo.model_copy(
2106	                update={
2107	                    "along_t_start": new_along_start,
2108	                    "along_t_end": new_along_end,
2109	                    "sill_height_mm": new_sill,
2110	                    "head_height_mm": new_head,
2111	                }

host_roof_id=cmd.host_roof_id,
                boundary_mm=cmd.boundary_mm,
            )

        case CreateText3dCmd():
            tid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if tid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{tid}'")
            if not cmd.text:
                raise ValueError("createText3d.text must be a non-empty string")
            els[tid] = Text3dElem(
                kind="text_3d",
                id=tid,
                text=cmd.text,
                font_family=cmd.font_family,
                font_size_mm=cmd.font_size_mm,
                depth_mm=cmd.depth_mm,
                position_mm=cmd.position_mm,
                rotation_deg=cmd.rotation_deg,
                material_key=cmd.material_key,
            )

        case CreateWallOpeningCmd():
            oid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if oid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{oid}'")
            host = els.get(cmd.host_wall_id)
            if not isinstance(host, WallElem):
                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.hostWallId must reference a wall")
            if cmd.along_t_start >= cmd.along_t_end:
                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.alongTStart must be < alongTEnd")
            if cmd.head_height_mm <= cmd.sill_height_mm:
                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.headHeightMm must be > sillHeightMm")
            if cmd.head_height_mm > host.height_mm:
                raise ValueError("createWallOpening.headHeightMm must not exceed host wall height")
            els[oid] = WallOpeningElem(
                kind="wall_opening",
                id=oid,
                name=cmd.name,
                host_wall_id=cmd.host_wall_id,
                along_t_start=cmd.along_t_start,
                along_t_end=cmd.along_t_end,
                sill_height_mm=cmd.sill_height_mm,
                head_height_mm=cmd.head_height_mm,
            )

        case UpdateWallOpeningCmd():
            wo = els.get(cmd.opening_id)
            if not isinstance(wo, WallOpeningElem):
                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening.openingId must reference a wall_opening")
            host_wall = els.get(wo.host_wall_id)
            if not isinstance(host_wall, WallElem):
                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening host wall missing")
            new_along_start = (
                cmd.along_t_start if cmd.along_t_start is not None else wo.along_t_start
            )
            new_along_end = cmd.along_t_end if cmd.along_t_end is not None else wo.along_t_end
            new_sill = cmd.sill_height_mm if cmd.sill_height_mm is not None else wo.sill_height_mm
            new_head = cmd.head_height_mm if cmd.head_height_mm is not None else wo.head_height_mm
            if new_along_start >= new_along_end:
                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening alongTStart must be < alongTEnd")
            if new_head <= new_sill:
                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening headHeightMm must be > sillHeightMm")
            if new_head > host_wall.height_mm:
                raise ValueError("updateWallOpening headHeightMm must not exceed host wall height")
            els[cmd.opening_id] = wo.model_copy(
                update={
                    "along_t_start": new_along_start,
                    "along_t_end": new_along_end,
                    "sill_height_mm": new_sill,
                    "head_height_mm": new_head,
                }
            )

        case CreateProjectBasePointCmd():
            if any(isinstance(e, ProjectBasePointElem) for e in els.values()):
                raise ValueError(
                    "createProjectBasePoint: a project_base_point already exists (singleton)"
                )
            pid = cmd.id or new_id()

+def _validate_wall_stack(components: list, wall_height_mm: float) -> None:
+    """KRN-V3-02: validate stacked-wall components.
+
+    Rules: at least one component; Σ heightMm of all but the last must be
+    strictly less than wall_height_mm (the last component fills the remainder).
+    """
+    if not components:
+        raise ValueError("wall stack must have at least one component")
+    prefix_sum = sum(c.height_mm for c in components[:-1])
+    if prefix_sum >= wall_height_mm:
+        raise ValueError(
+            f"wall stack prefix Σ heightMm ({prefix_sum} mm) must be < "
+            f"wall heightMm ({wall_height_mm} mm)"
+        )

class RoofJoinElem(BaseModel):
    """KRN-V3-03 G11 — derived overlay that joins two roof solids along a seam.

    Does not mutate the source RoofElem records. The renderer computes the seam
    polyline on the fly from the two RoofElem footprints.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["roof_join"] = "roof_join"
    id: str
    name: str = "Roof Join"
    primary_roof_id: str = Field(alias="primaryRoofId")
    secondary_roof_id: str = Field(alias="secondaryRoofId")
    seam_mode: Literal["clip_secondary_into_primary", "merge_at_ridge"] = Field(
        alias="seamMode"
    )
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")


class EdgeProfileRunElem(BaseModel):
    """KRN-V3-03 G12 — swept profile along a host element edge (fascia/gutter/cornice/plinth).

    ``hostEdge`` is one of the named edge tokens or a custom ``{startMm, endMm}`` dict.
    The renderer computes the swept solid; plan view shows a thin line on the edge.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["edge_profile_run"] = "edge_profile_run"
    id: str
    name: str = "Edge Profile Run"
    host_element_id: str = Field(alias="hostElementId")
    host_edge: Any = Field(alias="hostEdge")
    profile_family_id: str = Field(alias="profileFamilyId")
    offset_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="offsetMm")
    miter_mode: Literal["auto", "manual"] = Field(default="auto", alias="miterMode")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")


class SoffitElem(BaseModel):
    """KRN-V3-03 G13 — horizontal soffit panel under a roof eave.

    ``boundaryMm`` is a closed plan polygon (≥ 3 vertices). ``zMm`` is the
    underside elevation; the engine fills it from the host roof eave when the
    command omits it.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["soffit"] = "soffit"
    id: str
    name: str = "Soffit"
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    host_roof_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hostRoofId")
    thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="thicknessMm")
    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _validate_boundary(self) -> SoffitElem:
        if len(self.boundary_mm) < 3:
            raise ValueError("SoffitElem.boundaryMm must have ≥ 3 vertices")
        return self

    polyline on the fly from the two RoofElem footprints.

    """KRN-V3-03 G13 — horizontal soffit panel under a roof eave.

    underside elevation; the engine fills it from the host roof eave when the
    command omits it.

    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")
